2604.00923.title: Positional Cognitive Specialization: Where Do LLMs Learn To Comprehend and Speak Your Language?
2604.00923.paper_summary: Adapting large language models (LLMs) to new languages is an expensive and opaque process. Understanding how language models acquire new languages and multilingual abilities is key to achieve efficient adaptation. Prior work on multilingual interpretability research focuses primarily on how trained models process multilingual instructions, leaving unexplored the mechanisms through which they acquire new languages during training. We investigate these training dynamics on decoder-only transformers through the lens of two functional cognitive specializations: language perception (input comprehension) and production (output generation). Through experiments on low-resource languages, we demonstrate how perceptual and productive specialization emerges in different regions of a language model by running layer ablation sweeps from the model's input and output directions. Based on the observed specialization patterns, we propose CogSym, a layer-wise heuristic that enables effective adaptation by exclusively fine-tuning a few early and late layers. We show that tuning only the 25% outermost layers achieves downstream task performance within 2-3% deviation from the full fine-tuning baseline. CogSym yields consistent performance with adapter methods such as LoRA, showcasing generalization beyond full fine-tuning. These findings provide insights to better understand how LLMs learn new languages and push toward accessible and inclusive language modeling.
2604.00923.paper_summary_zh: 適應大型語言模型 (LLMs) 到新語言是一個昂貴且不透明的過程。理解語言模型如何獲得新語言和多語言能力是實現高效適應的關鍵。先前的多語言可解釋性研究主要集中在訓練模型如何處理多語言指令上，卻未探索它們在訓練過程中獲得新語言的機制。我們通過兩個功能性認知專門化的視角來研究這些訓練動態：語言感知（輸入理解）和產出（輸出生成）。通過對低資源語言的實驗，我們展示了感知和產出專門化如何在語言模型的不同區域中出現，方法是從模型的輸入和輸出方向進行層消融掃描。根據觀察到的專門化模式，我們提出了 CogSym，一種層級啟發式方法，通過專門微調少數早期和晚期層來實現有效的適應。我們顯示僅調整最外層的 25% 層能在下游任務中達到與全面微調基準相差 2-3% 的性能。CogSym 與 LoRA 等適配器方法表現出一致的性能，展示了超越全面微調的泛化能力。這些發現為更好地理解 LLMs 如何學習新語言提供了見解，並推進可訪問和包容的語言建模。
2604.00923.author: Luis Frentzen Salim et.al.
2604.00923.authors: Luis Frentzen Salim, Lun-Wei Ku, Hsing-Kuo Kenneth Pao
